title: "EasyUbuntu and ATI driver support - or lack of?"

forum: Desktop Environments
---

### Post by Sslaxx on 2006-08-14
Well, I used the EasyUbuntu repos to install the ATI fglrx drivers and configured them... except they haven't been.

fglrxinfo reports:```
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: Mesa project: www.mesa3d.org
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect
OpenGL version string: 1.2 (1.5 Mesa 6.4.1)
```

And xorg.conf looks like:```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"

	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "gb"
EndSection

	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"

EndSection

EndSection

EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. RV280 [Radeon 9200]"

	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"

	SubSection "Display"

	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"

	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"

EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

Any ideas, anyone?

---

### Post by Malac on 2006-08-14
It looks like you are one of the unlucky ones, the FGRLX driver has "flaky" support for older/lower cards (thats anything less than 9600 according to the posts I've found.
It seems to be hit and miss whether it works on RV280/9200 cards or not.
Some threads suggests using the older 8.24.8 driver as this seems to work.

Hope this helps.

---

It seems to be hit and miss whether it works on RV280/9200 cards or not.
Some threads suggests using the older 8.24.8 driver as this seems to work.

Hope this helps.
Great. Looks like that was a waste of time, then. OGRE experimentation will have to wait until I can afford a more modern PC/graphics card. Thanks anyway.

### Post by WalmartSniperLX on 2006-08-14
Do you have **all** the repositories open? I had THE same exact problem the first time I installed the driver. But after thinking things through, there is a command you type in that requires an app to be installed (and if you arent connected to the right channel, you cannot retrieve it)

 You need to be connected to all ur software channels (just for assurance) in the repositories. After that give it another try and it should give you what you need. 

-Goto System/Administration/Software Manager
-Check all the boxes that arent already checked
-Exit (click ok or whatever) and click reload
-NOTE: Before redoing the install procedure make sure ur driver installed now is uninstalled
-Follow same install procedures completely
-Check if it worked 

Just in case, heres the instructions I used: [http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=204910](http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=204910)

I dont wanna assume this is the problem (or if you already did this) but just incase Im posting this. 


EDIT: **The latest driver supports anything from the 8500 and up.** Download it here [https://a248.e.akamai.net/f/674/9206/0/www2.ati.com/drivers/linux/ati-driver-installer-8.27.10-x86.run](https://a248.e.akamai.net/f/674/9206/0/www2.ati.com/drivers/linux/ati-driver-installer-8.27.10-x86.run) When using the instructions I posted, just make sure you replace the driver build (8.16.18 ) with 8.27.10 

And remember this is the 32bit driver (x86). A 64bit driver is available.

---
